finding_results.notes: Lena now states she is testing for a first-second physical lead-in, explains that prior pool knowledge was only post-hoc telemetry reconstruction, defines the stillness/random-cue/outside witness difference, and Kai reports only what he directly saw before naming the remaining uncertainty about whether the roadside result matches the pool episodes.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_07.md:85; chapters/chapter_07.md:97; chapters/chapter_07.md:111; chapters/chapter_07.md:149; chapters/chapter_07.md:173; chapters/chapter_07.md:199; chapters/chapter_07.md:297; chapters/chapter_07.md:453; chapters/chapter_07.md:459
finding_results.notes: Key admissions are now built through stoppage, self-correction, and object-grounding rather than aphoristic diagnosis: Lena revises her account of the program and home, Kai grounds his ownership fear in the wrist and shoulder, and the late outdoor admissions arrive messily instead of as polished tags.
The dinner and walk no longer re-prove the brace as a repeated visual reminder. Post-opening brace references now stay tied to the tactile demonstration scene and its mechanics.
Vague placeholder uses were replaced with specific physical or situational language. The chapter now retains only one instance of the word 'something' in ordinary dialogue.

summary: Revised the dinner and mountain-road scenes to roughen composed vulnerability beats, clarify the roadside test's logic and evidentiary limits, remove redundant brace reminders, and cut 'something' usage well below the packet threshold.
